source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Devils Tower Platform Walkway Detachment

context.region: Gulf of America, Mississippi Canyon Block 773

event.top_event: Structural failure and detachment of external walkway platform
actions_taken: Three employees evacuated to safe locations
Walkway and stairway secured with chain bindings
Affected area cordoned off with red danger tape
Third-floor personnel relocated to alternative accommodations
All walkway tabs rewelded with correct support (completed by 09 September 2025)
NDT inspections scheduled for repaired welds
event.summary: On 16 July 2025 at 1810 hours, an external walkway platform on the third floor of the Temporary Living Quarters (TLQ) on Devils Tower SPAR platform unexpectedly detached from the TLQ structure by 12 to 18 inches while three Nabors employees were standing on it. The walkway remained partially secured by stanchions and stairway bolts. All personnel safely evacuated. Investigation revealed the TLQ, originally designed as temporary housing in 2002, had remained on site for 23 years. The welded tabs securing the walkway lacked sufficient welded surface area and were subject to fatigue and corrosion in the marine environment. Visual-only inspections in May 2024 failed to detect the deteriorated weld condition.
recommendations: BSEE recommends issuing a Safety Alert regarding the incident
Eni to ensure future design and construction of temporary structures account for potential long-term use
Eni to incorporate dedicated inspections and protocols for TLQs focusing on marine corrosion, fatigue, and weld conditions
Implement specific annual inspection requirements for TLQ landings and tab welds in Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS)
Conduct Level 1 surveys with enhanced inspection criteria that account for age and environmental degradation
key_phrases: temporary structure left in place 23 years

hazards.name: Structural degradation of temporary living quarters
Marine corrosion and fatigue of welded connections
Inadequate structural design for long-term use

threats.name: Fatigue and material degradation of welded tabs due to corrosion
Insufficient welded surface area on connection tabs
Lack of cross bracing on walkway structure
Failure to detect weld defects through visual inspection only

consequences.name: Walkway platform detachment from TLQ structure
Property damage to walkway and support structures

controls.name: Welded tabs securing walkway to TLQ structure
controls.barrier_role: Structural connection to prevent walkway separation
controls.lod_basis: Primary structural support for walkway attachment

supporting_text: the welded tabs securing the walkway to the TLQ structure were not sufficient to provide a long-term permanent attachment point
The tabs lacked welded surface area which would have provided additional support for the tabs
the method in which the tabs were welded was meant to simplify any future demolition or relocation of the TLQ

controls.name: Cross bracing on walkway structure
controls.barrier_role: Structural reinforcement to distribute loads and prevent excessive stress
controls.lod_basis: Secondary structural support to reduce stress concentration

supporting_text: a lack of cross bracing on the south side of the walkway could have contributed to additional stress and structural vulnerability of the failed section

controls.name: Visual inspection of TLQ structural components
controls.barrier_role: Detection of structural defects and degradation
controls.lod_basis: Inspection to identify hazards before failure

supporting_text: a visual inspection was performed on the TLQ. This visual inspection was deemed by Eni to be inadequate to detect welding defects
Because Eni only performed a visual inspection of the TLQ's structural components, the inspection was unable to identify the deteriorated condition of the welded tabs
the inspections that were performed did not take the age of the TLQ into consideration

controls.name: Level 1 structural survey of platform components

supporting_text: The most recent Level 1 survey that was conducted in May 2024 did not indicate any discrepancies in the walkways or structures for the TLQ. This was most likely due to only visual inspections having been performed

controls.name: Immediate securing of detached walkway with chain bindings
controls.barrier_role: Prevent further separation and dropped objects

supporting_text: Immediately after all individuals were in safe locations, the stairway and walkway were secured with chain bindings to prevent any dropped objects or the walkway separating even further
Due to the immediate action taken to secure the walkway, there were no injuries or dropped objects associated with the incident

controls.name: Cordoning off and warning signage of affected area
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel access to hazardous area

supporting_text: the affected area was cordoned off, and red danger tape was installed around the area to warn personnel of the hazards in the area

controls.name: Relocation of personnel to alternative accommodations

supporting_text: All personnel that were housed on the third floor living quarters were then relocated to alternative accommodations, using an alternative walkway

controls.name: Rewelding of walkway tabs with correct support
controls.barrier_role: Restore structural integrity of walkway attachment
controls.lod_basis: Corrective action to restore failed barrier

supporting_text: Per Eni, as of 09 September 2025, all tabs have been correctly welded to provide the correct amount of support for the walkways on the TLQ

controls.name: Non-Destructive Testing (NDT) inspection of repaired welds
controls.barrier_role: Verify integrity of repaired welds before return to service

supporting_text: Eni is still awaiting NDT inspections for those repairs to place the walkways back in full service. Projected timeframe for completion of the NDT is 15 October 2025

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
